data.body: Root cause CONFIRMED on current main: route_inbound_probe (reconcile.py:317-330) builds the inbound-delete Mutation with payload {'reason':'hard_delete', ...} but _apply_inbound_delete (apply_inbound.py:184) reads payload.get('probe_outcome', 'out_of_window') -> the hard_delete branch is UNREACHABLE (always defaults to out_of_window); the create_after_hard_delete follow_on (apply_inbound.py:202-208) is built but the applier consumer (applier.py:467-469) only handles kind=='suppress_pair', so it's dropped. Fix plan: (1) leaf branch = payload.get('reason') or payload.get('probe_outcome','out_of_window'); update fixtures test_inbound_leaf_bodies.py (redirect + hard_delete). (2) applier consumer: on follow_on action=='create_after_hard_delete', look up the still-present LOCAL ticket by local_id, reconstruct create fields via outbound_fields._map_local_to_jira_fields, build a standard outbound CREATE Mutation, and add it to the pass's outbound mutation list so it flows through create_one (JQL dedup + bind_pending/bind_confirm) — no mid-loop create_one, no typed-dispatch bypass. (3) binding_store: on rebind, drop stale reverse[old_key] in the same save. (4) idempotency: JQL dedup prevents double-create; REST-budget exhaustion defers to next pass. (5) guard so only genuine hard_delete re-creates; repoint TODO(epic-3e36) at apply_inbound.py; clear .joe-janitor/report flag. Not yet implemented.
